title: [CI] Meta task not updating EC2 images
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

For `main` and all release branches v4.0 and more recent utilize AWS EC2 for podman machine testing.  However, the meta task has not been updated with EC2 support.  Within 30-days of inactivity, EC2 images may be culled.  This would be disastrous for release-branches.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Ignore it

**Describe the results you received:**

Meta task does not update metadata on EC2 AMIs used for testing

**Describe the results you expected:**

Meta task updates all images used by CI on the branch.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

N/A

**Output of `podman info`:**

N/A

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

N/A

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The current (as of opening this issue) version of the imgts container supports both AMI and image name-tag references.  However, this is only activated if the `EC2IMGNAMES` (newline separated) list of images is set, along with an `AWSINI` encrypted value.
